Look it’s a nice proof of concept, but that’s basically it… the speech to text rarely ever gets it right, for example trying to say Mika would translate to Me Pizza or something random like that- and even with the lack of filters, the ai still repeats things… and when you say “Please Sit” they ai will flat out sit in the air-

Waifu skins don’t save when changing scale or change scenes… and sometimes mods will mess with the description, so you could load in a Hatsune Miku character, but a Monika description would still be in place… 

The characters also seem to react too much at times, for example giving a character a high five could suddenly cause the character to think it’s time for something NSFW- 

The other thing is the crashes, it crashes a lot… it could be when loading characters, when loading a scene, or just simply changing a setting like the text to speech option or the scaling- 

The characters are also pretty static, not very expressive, and not very life like- but as I said before, it’s a nice proof of concept. This app would of only gotten 2 stars, however it’s getting one extra star for the ragdoll physics- it would of been another star if this was a free application, but it cost about 15 dollars a month for unlimited mode-


TLDR : Nice proof of concept, but very Buggy and Unpolished…

reviews.review_title: I see the idea of what they're doing.
reviews.review_description: I think what they're doing with this game is what people seek out of their technology. This would most certainly be a killer-app if there weren't so many bugs (and that's no shade at the dev). The game needs more time, also if the AI were more advanced, which I know is no small task, this app would be a must for people who want an ideal VR experience that doesn't involve workspace tasks. I recommend it as a Quest companion app. (which should be its own category of apps type). That would keep the people who complain about the apps level of appropriate-"ness" to a minimum.

You are in charge of a blank sheet of paper, you begin to write your story, but the paper writes back. The AI responses, and overall design have a pretty solid lil game, but tread carefully. The more you invest time into this game the more your game will change. It can be overwhelming for first time VR users and a ten year old with a decent amount of time can create a raving maniac but that's kind of the point. Have fun and if your creation isn't what you want you can always delete... The work is so impressive I've signed up to support the creators Patreon, I'm curious to see where this goes!
